front.title: Portrait of Ho Chi Minh
front.description: Every modern Vietnamese banknote features Ho Chi Minh, the founding father of modern Vietnam. His portrait symbolizes national unity, independence, and the country's revolutionary history. Around the portrait are advanced anti-counterfeiting features including watermarks, transparent windows, holograms, microprinting, and other security elements depending on the denomination.

polymer.description: Introduced in 2003 to improve durability, cleanliness, and counterfeit resistance. These denominations represent the daily financial lifeblood of modern Vietnam and are the notes tourists encounter most frequently.

cotton.description: These smaller paper notes remain in circulation primarily for inexpensive purchases, street markets, parking, public restrooms, and exact change.

bills.label: 500,000 VND

back.title: Kim Lien Village
back.description: Kim Lien is the birthplace and childhood home of Ho Chi Minh. The village reflects his humble beginnings before becoming Vietnam's revolutionary leader.

bills.culture: The highest commonly circulated Vietnamese banknote honors the birthplace of Ho Chi Minh. It symbolizes respect for Vietnam's origins, humility, and the early life of the nation's most influential leader, reinforcing the importance of remembering one's roots.
bills.tip: Commonly used for hotels, larger restaurants, shopping, and higher-value purchases. Many small vendors may not have enough change.

bills.label: 200,000 VND

back.title: Dinh Huong Islet (Incense Burner Islet)
back.description: The famous limestone islet located within the UNESCO World Heritage Site of Ha Long Bay.

bills.culture: The note celebrates one of Vietnam's most iconic natural landscapes. Ha Long Bay represents the country's natural beauty, geological heritage, and national pride, while also highlighting one of Vietnam's most internationally recognized destinations.
bills.tip: Ideal for restaurants, groceries, taxis, excursions, and medium-sized purchases.

bills.label: 100,000 VND

back.title: Temple of Literature (Văn Miếu - Quốc Tử Giám)
back.description: Founded in 1070, this was Vietnam's first national university and remains one of the country's most important cultural and educational monuments.

bills.culture: The Temple of Literature embodies Vietnam's deep respect for education, scholarship, Confucian values, and lifelong learning. It reflects centuries of academic tradition and cultural development.
bills.tip: A very practical everyday note for meals, transportation, shopping, and attractions.

bills.label: 50,000 VND

back.title: Nghenh Luong Pavilion & Phu Van Lau
back.description: Historic Nguyen Dynasty buildings located beside the Perfume River in Vietnam's former imperial capital.

bills.culture: The note celebrates Vietnam's imperial heritage through the architecture of the Nguyen Dynasty. It reflects the country's royal history, traditional architecture, and the historical significance of Hue as the imperial capital.
bills.tip: Excellent for cafés, museums, local restaurants, and short taxi rides.

bills.label: 20,000 VND

back.title: Japanese Covered Bridge (Chùa Cầu)
back.description: Constructed by Japanese merchants during the 17th century, this bridge has become the symbol of Hoi An Ancient Town.

bills.culture: The bridge represents centuries of international trade, cultural exchange, and cooperation between Vietnamese, Japanese, Chinese, and European merchants. It symbolizes Vietnam's long history as an important trading nation.
bills.tip: Very common for street food, drinks, markets, cafés, and convenience stores.

bills.label: 10,000 VND

back.title: Bach Ho (White Tiger) Oilfield
back.description: One of Vietnam's largest offshore oil fields and an important contributor to the country's economic development.

bills.culture: The note represents Vietnam's industrial modernization, offshore energy production, and maritime economy. It reflects the nation's progress in developing modern industries while emphasizing economic growth.
bills.tip: Useful for small meals, street food, drinks, parking, and exact change.

bills.label: 5,000 VND

back.title: Tri An Hydropower Plant
back.description: One of Vietnam's major hydroelectric facilities supplying electricity to southern Vietnam.

bills.culture: The hydropower plant symbolizes Vietnam's modernization through electrification, infrastructure development, and industrial growth, reflecting the country's investment in sustainable energy production.
bills.tip: Useful for parking, small snacks, local markets, and exact payments.

bills.label: 2,000 VND

back.title: Nam Dinh Textile Factory
back.description: Depicts female textile workers at one of Vietnam's historic industrial factories.

bills.culture: The note honors Vietnam's industrial workforce, particularly women who played a significant role in the country's manufacturing sector. It celebrates labor, perseverance, and economic development.
bills.tip: Mainly useful for exact change and rounding small purchases.

bills.label: 1,000 VND

back.title: Timbering Operation with Elephants
back.description: Depicts traditional logging work using elephants, a historical practice in Vietnam's Central Highlands.

bills.culture: This note highlights Vietnam's rural workforce, forestry traditions, and the cultural heritage of indigenous communities in the Central Highlands. Although this practice has largely disappeared today, it represents an important part of Vietnam's historical relationship with its forests and local cultures.
bills.tip: Rarely needed by tourists but useful for exact cash totals.
